content.full_text:
Commission Staff:

The league's women believe the
San Diego is studying
the government of the San
Diego League Port District
(STATS. 1962, 1st Ex.Sess.,c.60)
And we wonder if the
Port Commission are
slicked up in the Facie
Political Practice let. We
hope that it something you
can inform us about.

OLIVE WETHERING
1210 Governor Drive
San Diego, CA 92122

2 Nov 96

4219 Governor Drive
San Diego, CA 92122
November 24, 1990

Steve Rhoads
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Rhoads:

On Nov. 2, 1990, I mailed a communication to the FPPC. A Nov. 21
reply by Scott Hallabrin refers to it as "Letter No. 90-696" although,
as I recall, I dashed it off on a postal card. Mr. Hallabrin wrote me,
"If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact
Steve Rhoads." So let me explain.

A committee of eight from the League of Women Voters of San Diego
is studying the San Diego Unified Port District with not much more in
mind than to learn as much as we can about this agency in the short
span of four months. Early in December we shall boil down what we have
learned into a 4-8-page publication that will be distributed to all
members of our League for their information.

I go into this detail to show you that fair political practices
was something we thought we should check out. The easiest way to do
it seemed to be to ask the FPPC if members of the Board of Port
Commissioners, citizens appointed by their city councils, were subject
to the Fair Political Practices Act (or perhaps the Political Reform
Act which Mr. Hallabrin mentioned).

We expected the answer to be, "No." If, by any chance, the
commissioners are subject to a fair political practices law, we
expected a staff person from the FPPC to let us know in what law,
section, and/or code we could find the reference which we could then
look up ourselves. We might put the information in our publication.
At least we'd have authenticated information we could keep on file
with the background papers for the publication, in case anyone asks
us the question we asked the FPPC: Are the members of the Board of
Port Commissioners subject to the Fair Political Practices Act?

The San Diego Unified Port District Act was passed by the
Legislature in 1962 (Stats. 1962, 1st Ex. Sess., c. 67, as amended
by Stats. 1963, c.673 and other amendments that do not apply to the
commissioners).

If I do not hear from you before our next committee meeting on
November 29, I'll call you to see what steps we should take to find
this information. My telephone number is 619-453-6389.

Sincerely yours,

Olive Wehbring
Olive Wehbring

We hope that is something you can inform us about. A Nov. 21 reply by Scott Hallabrin refers to it as "Letter No. 90-696" although, as I recall, I dashed it off on a postal card. Mr. Hallabrin wrote me, "If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact Steve Rhoads." So let me explain. A committee of eight from the League of Women Voters of San Diego is studying the San Diego Unified Port District with not much more in mind than to learn as much as we can about this agency in the short span of four months. The easiest way to do it seemed to be to ask the FPPC if members of the Board of Port Commissioners, citizens appointed by their city councils, were subject to the Fair Political Practices Act (or perhaps the Political Reform Act which Mr. Hallabrin mentioned). We expected the answer to be, "No." If, by any chance, the commissioners are subject to a fair political practices law, we expected a staff person from the FPPC to let us know in what law, section, and/or code we could find the reference which we could then look up ourselves. In case anyone asks us the question we asked the FPPC: Are the members of the Board of Port Commissioners subject to the Fair Political Practices Act? The San Diego Unified Port District Act was passed by the Legislature in 1962 (Stats. 1962, 1st Ex. Sess., c. 67, as amended by Stats. 1963, c. 673 and other amendments that do not apply to the commissioners). If I do not hear from you before our next committee meeting on November 29, I'll call you to see what steps we should take to find this information. My telephone number is 619-453-6389. Sincerely yours, Olive Wehbring
